steps.title: Identify the missing piece
steps.content:
The research question asks whether perceived category similarity predicts purchase likelihood.

Grasby et al. already have a measure of actual purchase likelihood (the probability changes they calculated for each extended-brand pair), but they need a corresponding measure of how similar consumers perceive those categories to be.

steps.title: Match the design to the research question
steps.content:
Choice A directly measures perceived category similarity for each extended-brand pair and correlates it with the probability changes—the exact comparison needed to test whether category similarity predicts purchase behavior.

Choices B, C, and D instead measure within-category similarity, brand recognition, and product cost respectively, none of which answer the research question.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
